payload.description: Unify init wizard rendering, make review side effects truthful, and improve terminal fallback and reinitialization guidance

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution
Not executed: user explicitly requested to ignore Claude Code

checklist.note: The plan omits coverage for the uninstall behavior affected by the new backup policy.
Backup disclosure is derived from file existence rather than whether the batched transaction will change the file.
The declared files cover the proposed implementation and tests.

findings.summary: Populating `backup_paths` from config-file existence falsely promises a backup for an already-current config, although the batched transaction is explicitly idempotent and creates none. The plan must derive disclosure from an actual pending settings change.

The zero-backup fresh-install design changes uninstall behavior without a test or implementation step: both adapters restore only from backups, so uninstalling a fresh installation will now leave all three managed hooks behind. Preserve or explicitly redesign the install/uninstall round trip.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution
Not executed: user explicitly requested to ignore Claude Code

checklist.note: The plan lacks a test for workspace or executable drift between preview and apply.
The preview used for user-visible backup disclosure is not bound to the later settings transaction.
The changed uninstall behavior requires updating an omitted user-facing adapter document.

findings.summary: Preflight records preview facts, but apply resolves the adapter and plans again. If the settings bytes or executable paths change while the user reviews the plan, apply can create an undisclosed backup or write different hook commands. Bind the frozen settings plan to apply, or revalidate its original bytes and commands before writing; add a drift regression test.

The scope changes no-backup uninstall from leaving the settings file untouched to marker removal/deletion, but Task 4 omits docs/adapters/claude-code.md, which explicitly documents the old behavior and calls it an open limitation. Add that document to scope and update its uninstall contract.

payload.reason: Configured two-round automatic cap reached; both Codex findings were addressed by freezing and drift-validating the settings transaction and adding adapter docs; user approved plan-review override and requested Claude Code be ignored
automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution
User explicitly requested to ignore Claude Code for this lifecycle.

checklist.note: The reviewed plan can conceal deletion of an existing review profile, and settings-path safety does not cover symlinked parent directories.
All changed files are declared in the plan scope.
The plan/apply action model does not represent every destructive operation truthfully.
Symlinked integration directories can redirect writes outside the workspace.
Documentation claims workspace-local symlink safety more broadly than the implementation provides.

findings.summary: When an existing `.harness/review-profiles.local.yaml` will be removed because the accepted configuration has no automated producers, `_review_file_actions` records the operation as UPDATE with `content=None`. The review therefore promises an update, but `_configure_review_producers` unlinks the file during apply. This violates the review-before-writes contract and conceals a destructive operation; the plan needs an explicit delete action rendered as such.

The safety check rejects only a symlink at the final settings filename. If `.codex` or `.claude` is itself a symlink, `hooks.json`/`settings.local.json` is not reported as a symlink and planning, locking, backup, and atomic replacement all operate outside the workspace. The install ownership check also accepts this because both paths resolve to the same external target. Reject symlinks in the path ancestry or enforce containment within the requested workspace before applying.

checklist.note: codex: The plan/apply action model does not represent every destructive operation truthfully.
codex: Documentation claims workspace-local symlink safety more broadly than the implementation provides.
codex: Symlinked integration directories can redirect writes outside the workspace.
codex: All changed files are declared in the plan scope.
codex: The reviewed plan can conceal deletion of an existing review profile, and settings-path safety does not cover symlinked parent directories.

findings.summary: The safety check rejects only a symlink at the final settings filename. If `.codex` or `.claude` is itself a symlink, `hooks.json`/`settings.local.json` is not reported as a symlink and planning, locking, backup, and atomic replacement all operate outside the workspace. The install ownership check also accepts this because both paths resolve to the same external target. Reject symlinks in the path ancestry or enforce containment within the requested workspace before applying.

When an existing `.harness/review-profiles.local.yaml` will be removed because the accepted configuration has no automated producers, `_review_file_actions` records the operation as UPDATE with `content=None`. The review therefore promises an update, but `_configure_review_producers` unlinks the file during apply. This violates the review-before-writes contract and conceals a destructive operation; the plan needs an explicit delete action rendered as such.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution
User explicitly requested to ignore Claude Code for this lifecycle.

checklist.note: Human-only reconfiguration remains broken in the supported line-mode fallback.
Reviewer/model filtering is inconsistent between guided and line UIs.
Force reconfiguration through TERM=dumb or redirected-output line mode retains models for deselected producers.
Documentation promises reviewed profile deletion without noting that line mode cannot complete it.

findings.summary: The guided UI now filters persisted models to selected producers, but the supported LineInitUI fallback still copies every persisted model. During `init --force`, deselecting the existing automated producer returns `review_producers=()` with `review_models={'codex': ...}`, and `build_init_plan` aborts with `review model source 'codex' has no selected producer`. Consequently TERM=dumb or TTY-input/redirected-output users cannot review and apply the new human-only profile deletion. Apply the same selected-source filtering in line mode and cover this reconfiguration path.

prior_findings.note: Existing producer profiles are now represented as explicit DELETE actions and apply consumes the frozen action.
Adapter plans now bind the workspace root and reject symlinks throughout the settings path during planning, apply, locking, and uninstall.
